nodes.method: supervised fine-tuning followed by reinforcement learning
nodes.mechanism: inducing ``pseudo reasoning paths'' imitated from expert models

nodes.claim_text: ThinkLite-VL-72B achieves 79.7 accuracy on MathVista and improves average benchmark performance by 4.42 over the open-source SOTA.
ThinkLite-VL-7B improves average performance over Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct by 7% and reaches a SoTA score of 75.1 on MathVista.
ThinkLite-VL-7B and ThinkLite-VL-72B significantly outperform their base models across eight visual reasoning benchmarks.
The approach selects 11k challenging samples for RFT on Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct and 7.5k for Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct.
ThinkLite-VL achieves SoTA performance using an order of magnitude fewer training samples via RFT self-improvement without knowledge distillation.
nodes.method: reinforcement fine-tuning self-improvement without knowledge distillation

nodes.claim_text: Sample difficulty critically influences RFT effectiveness, and appropriately challenging examples can improve reasoning in low-data regimes.
The method improves over a single-agent self-reflection baseline on FaithEval, AmbigDocs, and RAMDocs with Llama3.3-70B-Inst.
Systems generally perform worse on RAMDocs than on FaithEval or AmbigDocs.
On FaithEval, the method achieves the highest performance and surpasses the concatenated-prompt baseline with both Llama3.3-70B and Qwen2.5-72B.
The conversational MLOps assistant reduces complexity and lowers barriers to entry for users across diverse technical skill levels.
nodes.method: LLM-based conversational agent system for MLOps

nodes.claim_text: The system uses a hierarchical modular architecture with specialized agents for pipeline orchestration, data management, and domain-specific knowledge integration.
nodes.method: hierarchical, modular multi-agent architecture with KFP, MinIO, and RAG agents
nodes.mechanism: hierarchical modular agent architecture with RAG-based domain knowledge integration

nodes.claim_text: The LLM-based conversational agent system is designed to enhance human-machine collaboration in MLOps.

Through iterative reasoning loops and context-aware processing, the system enables users with varying technical backgrounds to perform ML pipeline, data, and documentation tasks via conversational interfaces.
nodes.method: iterative reasoning loops and context-aware processing in a conversational interface
nodes.mechanism: iterative reasoning loops and context-aware processing

nodes.claim_text: Equal Max reward scaling causes a slight drop in overall accuracy for most models, except Qwen2.5-3B which remains comparable to the original setting.
nodes.method: reward scale variant (Equal Max) in RL tool learning

nodes.claim_text: Coarser reward granularity makes it harder for the model to achieve higher reward values during RL training.
nodes.method: reward granularity design in RL tool learning

nodes.claim_text: The approach improves performance by 17% over base models and 15% over SFT models across diverse benchmarks.
For Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct on Bamboogle, SFT400+GRPO outperforms the raw model in accuracy.
For Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct on Bamboogle, SFT4k+PPO improves accuracy over the raw model.
The paper defines judge efficacy using improvement ratios over reranking and greedy responses for critique-based refinement.
Majority-vote aggregation is computed for GSM8k and MATH but is not feasible for CHAMP because GPT-4o grades natural language responses without extracting a final answer.
nodes.mechanism: grading natural language responses without final-answer extraction
nodes.failure_mode: cannot apply majority-vote aggregation when final answers are not extracted

nodes.claim_text: The pairwise protocol's normalized helpfulness is compared against Llama-3.1 8B with judge prompt, the best reward model, and random reranking, averaged across generator models and datasets.
nodes.evaluation_protocol: averaged across generator models and datasets

nodes.claim_text: Chain-of-thought prompting gives GPT-4o-mini only a small accuracy gain over direct-answer prompting.
Hint prompts can improve performance for Claude-3.7-Sonnet, Gemini-2.0-Pro, and Doubao-1.5-Vision-Pro-32k by over 8 points, surpassing 35% accuracy.
InternVL3-78B is the top open-source MLLM at 27.7%, only 0.4 points behind Doubao-1.5-Vision-Pro-32k and ahead of GPT-4o and Claude-3.7-Sonnet.
Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct-RL outperforms its non-RL counterpart by 2.0 percentage points, reaching 28.0%.
Within the Qwen2.5-VL series, the 72B model only slightly outperforms the 7B model by 0.2 points.
Multi-hop reasoning can improve task accuracy, but often at the cost of much higher token consumption and latency than baseline methods.
nodes.failure_mode: exponential growth in token consumption and latency

nodes.claim_text: FinSearchBench-24 covers five months of market variation, multi-variable interactions across stock, policy, and industrial sectors, and over 1,500 multiple-choice questions, exceeding traditional static benchmarks.
nodes.temporal_property: market data variations over five months

nodes.claim_text: Standard RAG methods such as dense retrieval with query decomposition achieve only 32.7% perfect recall and 40.9% F1 on OTT-QA.
nodes.failure_mode: inefficient coordination of multi-source heterogeneous data

nodes.claim_text: HM-RAG achieves state-of-the-art average accuracy of 58.55% on CrisisMMD, improving over GPT-4o and a text-only Qwen2.5-72B variant despite using 7B parameters.
A 7B multimodal enhanced variant improves average accuracy by 2.3% over text-only Qwen2.5-72B, indicating better parameter efficiency.
HM-RAG reaches 93.73% average accuracy on ScienceQA, surpassing LLaMA-SciTune and GPT-4o.
On Task 1 of CrisisMMD, the method attains 72.06% accuracy and outperforms GPT-4o by 3.86%.
Disabling WA reduces average performance by 5.63%, with a larger 6.35% accuracy drop on grade 7-12 tasks.
Mem0 variants outperform the strongest RAG approach by reaching 67% and over 68% versus around 61%.
nodes.method: memory-centric architecture with graph-based memory variant

nodes.claim_text: Structured relational graph memory substantially outperforms other methods in temporal reasoning.
nodes.mechanism: structured relational graphs capture chronological relationships and event sequences

nodes.claim_text: Increasing context length in frontier models delays but does not solve the fundamental limitation of long-term consistency.
nodes.failure_mode: fixed context windows do not solve the fundamental limitation

nodes.claim_text: On LOCOMO, the methods improve single-hop, temporal, and multi-hop reasoning over the best-performing methods and greatly reduce p95 latency versus full-context baselines.
The method achieves 26% relative improvement in LLM-as-a-Judge over OpenAI, and graph memory adds about 2% higher overall score than the base configuration.
RLOO uses a leave-one-out baseline to reduce variance in policy gradient estimation.
nodes.task: a leave-one-out baseline to reduce the variance in policy gradient estimation

nodes.claim_text: MetaSpatial introduces a multi-tier reward system for 3D scene generation.
nodes.mechanism: format validation, physics-based verification, and GPT-4o-driven perceptual scoring

nodes.claim_text: The reward design adjusts rewards for truncated outputs and discourages excessive verbosity using scaled punishments beyond a length threshold.
Dynamic sampling can substantially improve training efficiency.
nodes.mechanism: filtering out samples with accuracy rates of 0 or 1 to retain valid gradient signals

nodes.claim_text: ChestX-Reasoner (7B) consistently outperformed other MLLMs across tasks, improving average RadRScore by 18% over Qwen2VL-7B.
nodes.method: step-by-step verification with process supervision in a radiology diagnosis MLLM
nodes.mechanism: process supervision from step-by-step reasoning chains

nodes.claim_text: ChestX-Reasoner achieved 3% overall improvement over the state-of-the-art CheXagent-3B despite using fewer training samples.
nodes.method: step-by-step verification with process supervision in a radiology diagnosis MLLM

nodes.claim_text: ChestX-Reasoner improved over CheXagent-3B on RaTEscore by 17%, from 0.529 to 0.621.
nodes.method: step-by-step verification with process supervision in a radiology diagnosis MLLM

nodes.claim_text: ChestX-Reasoner outperformed GPT-4o and MedDr-40B on reasoning ability by 5.8% and 34.0%, respectively.
nodes.method: step-by-step verification with process supervision in a radiology diagnosis MLLM

nodes.claim_text: ChestX-Reasoner outperformed its base model Qwen2VL-7B by 27% overall and GPT-4o by 24% on average.
nodes.method: step-by-step verification with process supervision in a radiology diagnosis MLLM

nodes.claim_text: GPT-4o performed worse than ChestX-Reasoner on binary, single, and multiple disease diagnosis accuracy.
After reinforcement learning fine-tuning, the model's performance remains nearly identical to the original VLM backbone, differing by only 0.1% on average.
nodes.method: Reasoning Generation Policy Optimization (RGPO) with text KL regularization

nodes.claim_text: GPT-4o cannot perform reasoning generation without a reasoning mode for mathematical logic and physical perception scenarios.
OmniGen outperforms MetaQuery with Sana as the generator on DPG-Bench by 0.4%.
MindOmni achieves 71% overall score on WISE and 83% overall score on GenEval, with state-of-the-art performance on WISE.
In the Filemanagement domain, GPT-4o had high tool-selection success but much lower end-task accuracy.
nodes.failure_mode: high tool success does not translate to high task accuracy

nodes.claim_text: Gemini-2.5-Pro was the highest-accuracy model tested, while Llama-4 was the lowest.
Closed-source models outperformed open-source models on Websearch, but the gap was smaller than in other tasks.
Visionary-R1 outperforms strong multimodal models on multiple visual reasoning benchmarks when trained with only reinforcement learning on 273K CoT-free visual QA pairs.
nodes.method: reinforcement learning on CoT-free visual question-answer pairs

nodes.claim_text: Simply applying reinforcement learning to a VLM by prompting a reasoning chain can cause shortcut learning from easy questions and reduce generalization to unseen distributions.
Mitigating shortcut learning depends on encouraging the model to interpret images before reasoning.
The model is trained to follow a caption-reason-answer output format, generating a detailed image caption before an extensive reasoning chain.
The RL-only model has significantly higher average RH-AUC than SFT+RL across different reasoning lengths.
nodes.method: RL-only vs SFT+RL multimodal reasoning models

nodes.name: RL-only vs SFT+RL multimodal reasoning models
nodes.id: Method:rl-only vs sft+rl multimodal reasoning models

nodes.claim_text: LLM-R1 and R1-OneVision exhibit inadequate reasoning-hallucination balance, with RH-AUC of only 0.46.
Ocean-R1-7B has an RH-AUC score of 0.63 on RH-Bench.
Top models achieve slightly less than 40% on IMO 2025, indicating progress but substantial room for improvement.
MathArena has evaluated over 50 models across seven competitions totaling 162 problems.
nodes.task: evaluation of LLMs on math competitions

nodes.claim_text: Existing benchmarks do not evaluate proof-writing capabilities.
Recurring math competitions can provide high-quality, challenging problems for real-time evaluation of LLMs.
nodes.task: real-time evaluation of LLMs on math problems

nodes.claim_text: MathArena is the first benchmark for proof-writing capabilities.
DeepSeek-V3, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, and GPT-4.1 achieve relatively strong results, with accuracies of 13.6%, 13.2%, and 12.9% respectively.
Majority voting provides only a modest improvement in accuracy, typically by a few percentage points.
PHYBench is original and challenging for measuring LLM reasoning using physics problems.
Gemini 2.5 Pro has the highest reported S_EED and ACC values in the efficiency table.
Pass@32 is substantially higher than pass@1 for the listed models.
Using GPT-4.1-mini instead of GPT-4.1 for database construction causes a 4.1% performance drop.
Using GPT-4.1-mini instead of GPT-4.1 for the Frame Inspect tool causes a 3.7% performance drop.
DVD with Qwen3-32B attains 57.3% accuracy and outperforms existing VLM approaches including GPT-4o and OpenAI o3.
Switching the reasoning model in agentic search to OpenAI o4-mini leads to a 5.8% drop.
Switching the reasoning model in agentic search to GPT-4o causes a 13.7% decline.
GPT-4o significantly underperforms by collapsing into the Simple Action pattern for 91.4% of queries.
nodes.mechanism: collapsing into the Simple Action pattern

nodes.method: DNA-LLM hybrid with GRPO reinforcement learning
nodes.mechanism: larger LLM backbones provide a more effective substrate for reinforcement learning refinement

nodes.claim_text: Claude models achieved 63-64% win rates with identical macro-averages of 0.73.
GPT-4o achieved a 57% win rate, outperforming Gemini 2.0 Flash and GPT-4o mini.
Most LLM evaluations in medicine rely on closed-form exam-style question answering, with only 5% incorporating real EHR data.
ViGoRL-3B achieves 62.9% accuracy on SAT-2, improving over the base model and Vanilla GRPO.
ViGoRL-3B outperforms SFT Direct on BLINK and RoboSpatial, and outperforms vanilla GRPO on BLINK.
At 7B scale, ViGoRL improves SAT-2 accuracy to 67.5%, indicating scalability across model sizes.
ViGoRL-7B outperforms Qwen2.5-VL-72B by 6.0% on SAT-2 while using only 1k distillation examples and being 10× smaller.

The best open-source model, Qwen2.5-VL-72B, scores 30.7%, below the top proprietary model.
GPT-5 achieves only 41.9% average accuracy, far below human performance above 97%, indicating a large human-model gap in spatial intelligence.
GPT-4o labels error types correctly for only 53% of selected samples, suggesting answer correctness alone is insufficient to assess reasoning quality.
